member.name: Chris Coghlan
member.synopsis: Chris Coghlan is the Liberal Democrat MP for Dorking and Horley , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Electronic Cigarettes: Advertising
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps the Government is taking to prevent the advertising and promotion of vaping products in ways that appeal to children and young people, including through the use of child-friendly brandin
writtenQuestions.answerText: It is incredibly worrying that 25% of 11 to 15 year olds have tried vaping. That is why we are committed to bringing about definitive and positive change to stop future generations from becoming hooked on nicotine. Evidence shows us that vapes appeal to c...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Mental Capacity: Medical Examinations
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, pursuant to the answer of 25 June 2026 to question 11966 entitled Mental Capacity, how many mental capacity assessments were (a) ordered by the Court of Protection to be carried out and (b) found to confirm a lac
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Court of Protection does not record how many mental capacity assessments are ordered by the court or how many assessments find that a person lacks capacity.The Court of Protection publishes statistics on applications and orders made under the Mental C...

writtenQuestions.heading: School Milk
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether he has considered the potential merits of extending free school milk entitlement to children in reception.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Nursery Milk Scheme is a statutory scheme which allows early years childcare settings to reclaim the cost of providing one-third of a pint of milk per day to children under the age of five years old who attend a setting for two or more hours per day. ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Recreation Spaces
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what progress she has made in fulfilling Commitment 86 in the 2025 Environmental Improvement Plan to make sure that everyone has access to green or blue spaces within a 15-minute walk
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government is committed to reducing physical and intangible barriers to accessing nature and ensuring everyone lives within a 15-minute walk of a green or blue space. As set out in the Environmental Improvement Plan (EIP), progress towards meeting th...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Employment Schemes: Older People
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, further to question 119633 entitled Employment Schemes: Older People, what plans he has to measure the effectiveness of the 50 PLUS Champions and Midlife MOT schemes in supporting jobseekers aged 50 and
writtenQuestions.answerText: On 28 May, the department published Evaluation evidence from policies targeted at claimants aged 50 plus - GOV.UK, which evaluated the effectiveness of Additional Work Coach Time for customers aged 50 and over, 50PLUS Champions and the digital Midlife MOT...

writtenQuestions.heading: Domestic Abuse: Criminal Proceedings
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what steps he is taking to help support women who have suffered domestic violence through the criminal justice system.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Our Violence Against Women and Girls Strategy, published in December 2025, sets out how we will prevent abuse, pursue perpetrators, and support victims. We are introducing a domestic abuse identifier at sentencing in criminal cases, rolling out Domestic A...

writtenQuestions.heading: Ophthalmic Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to support and expand vision rehabilitation services.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Under the Care Act 2014, local authorities have the duty to shape their care market and to commission a range of high-quality, sustainable, and person-centred care and support services to meet the diverse needs of all local people. This includes encouragi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Nature Conservation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what recent steps she has taken to protect 30% of land for nature by 2030.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK Government is committed to protecting 30% of land and sea in the UK by 2030 (30by30). We are developing a 30by30 Delivery Plan for land and inland water areas, which will set out how we will lead, support, and inspire action across England to deliv...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Sheltered Housing: Service Charges
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what assessment his Department has made of the potential implications for his policies of arrangements whereby service charges and council tax on retirement properties remain pay
writtenQuestions.answerText: In relation to service charges I refer the hon. Member to the answer given to Question UIN 4726 on 8 June 2026. With regard to council tax, when a property has been left empty following the death of its owner or occupant, it is exempt from council tax for...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Working Conditions: Temperature
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what assessment he has made of the potential merits introducing a maximum temperature for workplaces.
writtenQuestions.answerText: There is already a legal duty for employers to fully assess the risks to workers from extreme heat, and additionally to ensure a reasonable temperature for indoor workplaces. The Government is committed to ensuring workplaces are safe in the modern world ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Sports: Temperature
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what steps she has taken to ensure the safety of users of (a) 5-A-Side and 7-A-Side football pitches and (b) all outdoor sports facilities during red weather warnings for heat.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The safety and wellbeing of everyone participating in sport is absolutely paramount. The day-to-day management and operational safety of all outdoor sports facilities, including 5-a-side and 7-a-side football pitches, remains the legal responsibility of i...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Mental Capacity
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, how many mental capacity assessments were undertaken by the Court of Protection in the last three years; and, of these, how many came back confirming lack of capacity reported per year in each of the last three y
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Court of Protection does not carry out mental capacity assessments.Assessments of an individual’s capacity are carried out by medical professionals and submitted as supporting evidence for applications to the court.The Court of Protection statistics o...

writtenQuestions.heading: Special Educational Needs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, when her Department plans to publish inclusion base guidance for schools.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As a core component of our £3.7 billion high needs capital settlement, we will invest in a transformational expansion of inclusion bases. We are aware of many examples of inclusion bases in mainstream settings that offer high quality teaching, bespoke lea...

writtenQuestions.heading: Schools: Child Sexual Abuse Material
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what guidance her Department has issued to schools on criminals targeting schools and using AI tools to create child sexual abuse material of pupils.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member for Eastleigh to the answer of 2 June 2026 to Question 3915.

writtenQuestions.heading: School Teachers' Review Body
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, when her Department plans to publish and respond to the School Teachers’ Review Body report.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We recognise the importance of timely information for schools and will respond as soon as possible.

writtenQuestions.heading: Special Educational Needs: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what plans she has to review place funding for special schools.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department has published a schools white paper, Every Child Achieving and Thriving, alongside a consultation on special educational needs and disabilities (SEND) reform, called Putting Children and Young People First. We have also published a summary ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Sustainable Farming Incentive
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, if she will share the (i) June and (ii) September 2026 allocation of Sustainable Farming Incentive funding with the National Farmers' Union.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government will announce the budget for the 2026 Sustainable Farming Incentive shortly.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs
